preview_text:
The model favours Canada to win at 41%, substantially ahead of Switzerland's 28% win probability. That gap reflects a clear analytical edge to Canada in this neutral-venue matchup, though the underdog retains meaningful chances of victory.

The draw sits at 31%, making it the second-most-likely outcome. The relatively even split across all three results—no single probability dominates beyond 44%—indicates a competitive contest. The model suggests Canada should control play, but Switzerland's win probability is high enough that a draw remains plausible and a Swiss upset far from implausible.

The model and market diverge sharply here. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both assign Canada just 27%, a 13-percentage-point gap below the model's 41%, while both sources rate Switzerland at 44%—16 points higher than the model. The dashboard does not adjudicate between them, but the gap is substantial enough to merit note for any forecast user.
